But I should start making more time for socializing IRL right now ive noticed - thats easier to miss i find if its not around in the house for a period f.e.
  [Scott Northwolf]: @Andreas Leijonmarck I can relate to that...

generation.reply:
Brother that hit.

If rest is not on the calendar, burnout will put itself there real quick.

My non negotiable next week is space to breathe. Training, a little silence, and getting around good people in real life. That last one matters more than most men admit.

Love this, Lea.

Biggest challenge for me is staying locked in after the novelty wears off. First few days feel powerful. Then life swings. That’s when the standard gets revealed.

You’re doing it the right way. Run the system. Adjust in real time. Keep going.

post.author: Lea Newkirk
post.title: Who are you, really?
post.body: 2d  • General 💭Who are you, really?It’s wild to realize that so many of the things we call "just who I am" are actually just habits we built to feel safe once upon a time. We spend so much energy trying to fix the person we think we are, but maybe the real work is just letting go of the patterns that don't serve the person we’re becoming.

The shift happens when you stop trying to fix yourself and start dropping what was never you in the first place.

Powerful post, Lea.
